summary: Added validation to file directories in library properties.

description.text:
Description:
This PR introduces a validation check for user-entered library paths in the Library Properties dialog. It ensures that only valid paths are accepted (Absolute/ Relative), preventing users from saving incorrect paths.

Changes Made:
Real-time Validation:

- When a user types a path, the system checks whether it is absolute or relative.
- If the path is relative, it is resolved against the database’s current directory (getDatabasePath() in BibDatabaseContext).
- If the path is invalid, an error message is displayed in the UI immediately after typing.
- If the path is empty, no validation issues occur.

Validation on Save:

- When attempting to save, if any path is invalid, an error message is displayed.
- Until a correct path is entered, saving will not be allowed for General Tab.

Issue Fixed:
- Fixes [#11840](https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/issues/11840)
- Prevents users from entering non-existing absolute or relative paths.
- Ensures UI feedback is provided in real time to guide users.

Testing:

- Entered valid/invalid absolute paths and confirmed proper validation.
- Entered valid/invalid relative paths and confirmed resolution against the database directory.
- Verified that saving is only possible when all paths are valid.

### Mandatory checks

- Go through the list below. Please don't remove any items.
- [x] done; [ ] not done / not applicable

summary: Reduce tooltip delay from 2s to 500ms/1s for improved UX

description.text:
<!--
Please use the GitHub issue tracker only for bug reports and smaller suggestions for improvements.
Requests for completely new features, questions and general feedback is now handled at https://discourse.jabref.org.
Thanks!
-->

**Is your suggestion for improvement related to a problem? Please describe.**
Tooltips currently take around 2 seconds to appear, delaying the display of contextual help and making the interface feel less responsive.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Reduce the tooltip delay to 500ms or 1 second for quicker access to help.

**Additional context**

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5587b732-feb4-480d-adad-7c8338df92b3)

summary: Preferences search should not only search labels

description.text:
Currently, the search functionality only considers labels and their associated content. Other components, such as the items of combo boxes or additional input controls, are ignored. This limits the usability and effectiveness of the search feature.

Suggested Improvement: 
Extend the search feature to include text from all elements ensuring comprehensive search coverage across the entire preferences .

summary: Incorrect Deletion Notification When Canceling Entry Deletion

description.text:
### JabRef version

5.15 (latest release)

### Operating system

Windows

### Details on version and operating system

Windows 10

### Checked with the latest development build (copy version output from About dialog)

- [x] I made a backup of my libraries before testing the latest development version.
- [x] I have tested the latest development version and the problem persists

### Steps to reproduce the behaviour

1- Open JabRef.
2-Select an entry from the list
3-Click the Delete option in the menu.
4- A confirmation dialog appears asking if you want to delete the entry.
5-Click Keep entry or close the dialog using the X button.
5-Even though the entry is not deleted, JabRef still displays a notification stating that "Deleted -1 entry(s)".


### Appendix

...
<details>

<summary>Log File</summary>

```
Paste an excerpt of your log file here
```

</details>

summary: Abbreviation of a list of entries cause two uncaught exceptions

description.text:
### JabRef version

Latest development branch build (please note build date below)

### Operating system

Windows

### Details on version and operating system

Windows 11

### Checked with the latest development build (copy version output from About dialog)

- [x] I made a backup of my libraries before testing the latest development version.
- [x] I have tested the latest development version and the problem persists

### Steps to reproduce the behaviour

1. Create a new library.
2. Use "Web Search" to search for something (for example, “SW”).
3. Select all resulting entries and import them into your library.
4. Select all the imported entries
5. From the "Quality" tab, abbreviate the journal names to "dotless." If the error does not appear, abbreviate them to "shortUnique" and repeat these steps a couple of times. The exceptions do not occur consistently.

**Explanation:** 
I traced the code and found that the issue occurs because a `CompoundEdit` object, which is not thread-safe, is accessed concurrently. In the `AbbreviationAction` class, the abbreviate method creates multiple Callable tasks that use NamedCompound instance. When I changed the code to iterate over the entries in a regular loop (instead of collecting tasks in threads) the errors no longer occurred.

### Appendix

Exceptions:
**Note:** The first exception always happens, and the second one happens rarely.

## Changes 1. On paste: If it is possible to add this keyword, directly add it without requiring the user to press Enter | Before | After | | :---: | :---: | | | | 2. Double click on keyword: remove it and enter edit mode. | Before | After | | :---: | :---: | ||| 3. Reorder keywords by drag and drop | Before | After | | :---: | :---: | ||| 4. Alphabetic ordering of keywords. In case the list is already alphabetically ordered, add the new keyword in alphabetical order. Otherwise, add it to the end | Before | After | | :---: | :---: | ||| ## Notes This pull request is a new version of https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/pull/12632 but solve checkstyle errors and branch name. The old pull request is closed and ignored.
